quality-project-step-1-baseline.title: Step 1: Run the baseline OOP app
quality-project-step-1-baseline.prompt: Start from a working catalog baseline and keep the project runnable.
quality-project-step-1-baseline.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: Test, debug, and polish a multi-file OOP catalog without breaking inheritance or polymorphism.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

quality-project-step-1-baseline.starterCode:
from models.book_item import BookItem
from services.catalog import catalog_lines

items = [BookItem("Python", "Ana", 2)]
for line in catalog_lines(items):
    print(line)

quality-project-step-4-polymorphic-tests.title: Step 4: Extend tests to mixed subclasses
quality-project-step-4-polymorphic-tests.prompt: Add `VideoItem` and update the service test to prove polymorphism works.
quality-project-step-4-polymorphic-tests.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: Test, debug, and polish a multi-file OOP catalog without breaking inheritance or polymorphism.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

quality-project-step-4-polymorphic-tests.starterCode:
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, ".")

from models.book_item import BookItem
from services.catalog import catalog_lines, total_fees

items = [BookItem("Python", "Ana", 2), BookItem("SQL", "Ben", 1)]
assert catalog_lines(items) == ["Book: Python by Ana", "Book: SQL by Ben"]
assert total_fees(items) == 6
print("catalog service tests passed")

help.concept: Test, debug, and polish a multi-file OOP catalog without breaking inheritance or polymorphism.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.

quality-project-step-5-storage-tests.starterCode:
from models.book_item import BookItem
from services.catalog import catalog_lines, total_fees

items = [BookItem("Python", "Ana", 2)]
for line in catalog_lines(items):
    print(line)
print(total_fees(items))

sc-module-10-oop-quality-project-project.title: Check the design choice
sc-module-10-oop-quality-project-project.prompt: Why does this module 10 oop quality project require each step to keep all files, not only the file changed in that step?
sc-module-10-oop-quality-project-project.hint: Choose the option that keeps object responsibilities clear.

help.concept: Cumulative project steps must preserve the whole runnable app state.
help.hint_1: Look for the answer that reduces duplication without hiding behavior.
help.hint_2: A good OOP answer usually keeps behavior near the object that owns the data.

options.b: Because the next step starts from the full previous project state and must still run.
options.c: Because only README files are graded.
options.d: Because imports work even when files are missing.

project-story.title: Module 10 OOP Quality Project: project story
project-story.bodyMarkdown: This graded project is cumulative. Each step starts from the exact full solution of the previous step, so the project grows like a real multi-file app.
